name: Theobromine
description: 3,7-Dimethylxanthine. The principle alkaloid in Theobroma cacao (the cacao bean) and other plants. A xanthine alkaloid that is used as a bronchodilator and as a vasodilator. It has a weaker diuretic activity than theophylline and is also a less powerful stimulant of smooth muscle. It has practically no stimulant effect on the central nervous system. It was formerly used as a diuretic and in the treatment of angina pectoris and hypertension. (From Martindale, The Extra Pharmacopoeia, 30th ed, pp1318-9)
indication: theobromine is used as a vasodilator, a diuretic, and heart stimulant. And similar to caffeine, it may be useful in management of fatigue and orthostatic hypotension.
mechanismOfAction: Theobromine stimulates medullary, vagal, vasomotor, and respiratory centers, promoting bradycardia, vasoconstriction, and increased respiratory rate. This action was previously believed to be due primarily to increased intracellular cyclic 3′,5′-adenosine monophosphate (cyclic AMP) following inhibition of phosphodiesterase, the enzyme that degrades cyclic AMP. It is now thought that xanthines such as caffeine and theobromine act as antagonist at adenosine-receptors within the plasma membrane of virtually every cell. As adenosine acts as an autocoid, inhibiting the release of neurotransmitters from presynaptic sites but augmenting the actions of norepinephrine or angiotensin, antagonism of adenosine receptors promotes neurotransmitter release. This explains the stimulatory effects of xanthine derivatives such as theobromine and caffeine. Blockade of the adenosine A1 receptor in the heart leads to the accelerated, pronounced "pounding" of the heart upon caffeine intake.
pharmacodynamics: Theobromine, a xanthine derivative like caffeine and the bronchodilator theophylline, is used as a CNS stimulant, mild diuretic, and respiratory stimulant (in neonates with apnea of prematurity).

interactions.name: Sodium phosphate
interactions.description: Theobromine may increase the nephrotoxic activities of Sodium phosphate.

interactions.name: Vemurafenib
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Theobromine can be increased when it is combined with Vemurafenib.

interactions.name: Candesartan cilexetil
interactions.description: The risk or severity of hypotension can be increased when Candesartan cilexetil is combined with Theobromine.

interactions.name: Acemetacin
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Theobromine can be decreased when used in combination with Acemetacin.

interactions.name: Fluvoxamine
interactions.description: The metabolism of Theobromine can be decreased when combined with Fluvoxamine.

interactions.name: Mexiletine
interactions.description: The metabolism of Theobromine can be decreased when combined with Mexiletine.

interactions.name: Midostaurin

interactions.name: Tramadol
